name: Kuilima Cove
tagline: A tiny protected cove at Turtle Bay with beginner-friendly snorkeling and green sea turtles just offshore.
parking: Use the Turtle Bay Resort public beach access parking; free but can fill on weekends
bestTime: Morning for clearest water and turtle sightings; summer for calmest conditions
talkStory: This little cove is protected by rocks on both sides, so it stays calm when the rest of the North Shore is pounding. Perfect for kids and beginner snorkelers — you'll see reef fish and very likely turtles. It's right next to Turtle Bay Resort but it's a public beach, so don't let the resort vibe intimidate you. Small beach so it can feel crowded with just a few families. Grab fish tacos at the resort's Surfer, The Bar after.
